title: World’s hottest city is in UP: What’s making Banda so unbearably hot
content: Banda, a district in Uttar Pradesh's Bundelkhand region, has repeatedly recorded the world's highest temperatures, becoming a man-made heat island. Experts attribute this to barren terrain, vanishing green cover, relentless sand mining, and drying rivers, creating a vicious cycle of extreme heat.  This situation is exacerbated by dry westerly winds and a lack of adequate winter rainfall.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi creating 'Gayle-level fear', says Pathan as 59-sixes record nears
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 93 off 38 balls, including 10 sixes, kept Rajasthan Royals' playoff hopes alive.  Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan lauded the 15-year-old's maturity and off-side play, comparing his fear factor among bowlers to Chris Gayle's peak. Sooryavanshi is now threatening Gayle's IPL record of 59 sixes in a season.

title: Leaked audio catches Meta CEO explaining exactly why company is tracking employees
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg, in leaked audio from an April 30 all-hands meeting, confirmed the company is tracking employee keystrokes, mouse movements, and screen activity to train its AI models—and admitted Meta deliberately withheld details to protect its competitive edge. The audio, obtained by More Perfect Union, reveals Zuckerberg justified the surveillance by saying Meta employees are smarter than outside contractors. The disclosure came days before Meta began laying off 8,000 workers.

title: 'Not a pandemic emergency': WHO warns of high local Ebola risk, says global threat is low
content: The WHO has declared the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, citing a high risk of national and regional spread. While the global risk is low and it doesn't meet pandemic criteria, the agency warns the outbreak may have begun months ago and expects case numbers to rise.

title: AWS CEO just told engineers: We will need tons and tons of software developers
content: AWS CEO Matt Garman reassures that AI won't eliminate software engineers but will transform their roles. He emphasizes a shift from basic coding to system architecture and problem-solving. Despite recent layoffs, Amazon's demand for developers is accelerating, with plans to hire thousands of interns and entry-level engineers globally, focusing on new skillsets for next-gen systems.

title: 'Gave them car, 10 tola gold': Woman dies by suicide due to 'dowry' harassment
content: A 21-year-old woman allegedly died by suicide in Gwalior, with her family accusing her in-laws of dowry harassment and physical abuse. The family claims the in-laws demanded a car and withheld her 'Stridhan' shortly after the wedding. Police are investigating the incident, awaiting medical and forensic reports to register a case.

title: Google-parent Alphabet's $190 billion capex plan has a 'warning' for Nvidia
content: Google plans a massive $190 billion investment in AI infrastructure, a six-fold increase from 2022, signaling a strong push into custom silicon with its new TPU 8th generation. This strategic move aims to reduce reliance on third-party chip suppliers like Nvidia, enhancing both training and inference capabilities for its AI models and services.

title: Parle gets a ‘Melody’ boost: Shares rise 5%, hit upper circuit — but there's a twist
content: The widespread online attention surrounding the gift exchange seemingly led to speculative activity in the stock, despite there being no business link between the company and the confectionery brand. In fact, Parle Products is not a listed company.

title: Pushkar Jog breaks silence on Alia Bhatt’s viral Cannes 2026 video
content: Actor Pushkar Jog has come forward to address swirling rumors about Alia Bhatt being overlooked by photographers at Cannes. According to Jog, who was present to witness the event firsthand, the alleged neglect stemmed from the hectic nature of the festival, where photographers are often confined to designated spots.

title: Fact check: Was Aaron Rodgers seen with wife after Steelers contract?
content: Aaron Rodgers, the NFL veteran, recently signed a one year deal with the Pittsburgh Steelers, as retirement rumors dominated the headlines, sparking chaos among millions of fans. Amid all the speculations, the spotlight remains on Aaron Rodgers’ wife even as he continues to maintain privacy about his marital life. Through all of this, a picture of Aaron Rodgers with a woman has convinced millions that the identity of his wife has finally been revealed.

title: Ronda Rousey’s net worth 2026: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Ronda Rousey, a trailblazer in combat sports, is projected to have a net worth of nearly $14 million by 2026. Her fortune stems from lucrative UFC and WWE careers, acting, endorsements, and book sales. Rousey's impact on women's MMA and her transition to WWE cemented her status as a crossover star, ensuring her continued relevance and earning potential.

title: Oilers linked to Alex Tuch amid NHL free agency contract rumors
content: NHL trade rumors are heating up as free agency approaches, and Alex Tuch of the Buffalo Sabres is reportedly in the Edmonton Oilers’ crosshairs. Recent rumors suggest Tuch’s contract value is taking a hit due to his inconsistent play in the playoffs. It’s an opportunity for Edmonton, who are searching for top-six scoring depth and tight salary cap restrictions and roster restructuring needs.

title: Rafael Nadal and Mery Perello net worth 2026: Fortune and investments
content: Rafael Nadal and Mery Perello’s combined net worth in 2026 is estimated at nearly $260 million. Nadal earned most of his fortune through tennis prize money, endorsements, and business investments. The couple also owns luxury properties and runs successful projects like the Rafa Nadal Academy. Despite their huge wealth, they continue to live a quiet and private life in Mallorca, Spain.

title: Live-in relationships and the law in India: Rights, protections & what courts have ruled
content: The evolution of legal perception in India now includes the recognition of live-in relationships, offering vital protections primarily for women and their children. While these arrangements differ from traditional marriages, they present avenues for victims of domestic violence to seek help, ensure maintenance support, and affirm the legitimacy of their children.

title: Did Dhurandhar: Part 2 leak sensitive military operation details? HC asks Centre to examine
content: The Delhi High Court has directed the Centre and CBFC to review allegations from a military official claiming the film 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' may have exposed sensitive Indian military and intelligence operations. The court emphasized these concerns warrant consideration, even for fictional entertainment.

title: ‘Frustration manifesting in his temperament’: BJP attacks Rahul over ‘traitor’ remark on PM
content: BJP President J.P. Nadda slammed Rahul Gandhi for calling PM Modi and Amit Shah 'traitors' who 'sold India'. Nadda attributed Gandhi's remarks to electoral frustration, questioning if fighting Naxalism or protecting India's territory constitutes treason. Gandhi had earlier accused the BJP of attacking the Constitution and weakening public institutions.

title: Three years on, DKS looks to Congress high command as tussle with Siddaramaiah for CM post continues
content: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar dismissed speculation of a rift with Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, stating they will visit Delhi if summoned by the party high command. This comes after a meeting with Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge amid ongoing rumors about leadership tussle within the state government.

title: After five humbling defeats, Shubman Gill and co. to ditch raging turners at home
content: After debacles in home Tests against New Zealand and South Africa, India are set to move away from raging turners and return to traditional pitches with gradual wear and tear. Carefully selected venues and black/mixed soil surfaces are expected to shape India’s strategy. India will play Afghanistan in a one-off Test match from June 6.

title: 'Stored at -4°C, requires -80°C': Cops 'request' family to take Twisha Sharma's 'decomposing' body
content: A Bhopal family faces a grim dilemma: claim their daughter's body or pursue a second post-mortem amid decomposition fears. Twisha Sharma, 33, was found dead five months after her wedding. Her parents suspect foul play and demand an independent autopsy, but the mortuary's limited preservation facilities raise concerns about vital evidence.

title: Big boost for RCB! Phil Salt set for return ahead of IPL 2026 playoffs
content: Explosive opener Phil Salt is set to return for Royal Challengers Bengaluru ahead of the IPL 2026 playoffs after recovering from a finger injury. His return provides a significant boost to RCB's strong top-order, which struggled in his absence.

title: 'What's wrong with it?' On caste census, SC says govt should know the numbers
content: The Supreme Court has upheld the Centre's decision to conduct caste enumeration in the upcoming 2027 Census, stating there is "nothing wrong with it." A bench led by the Chief Justice emphasized that such data is crucial for the government to identify backward classes and implement effective welfare measures. This marks the first comprehensive caste count since 1931.

title: CBSE’s answer sheet portal chaos leaves lakhs of students stranded, frustrated and unheard
content: CBSE is facing severe criticism after its Class XII answer sheet access portal remained dysfunctional for over 24 hours despite repeated assurances that it was operational. Students across the country reported login failures, captcha errors and inaccessible pages while trying to apply for scanned copies of evaluated answer books. The board later admitted to technical glitches and temporarily withdrew the portal link. With lakhs of students awaiting re-evaluation decisions, frustration is mounting over CBSE’s preparedness, accountability and handling of the crisis.

title: India plans to send oil tankers through Hormuz for new supply amid Iran conflict disruptions
content: India is preparing to send ships through the Strait of Hormuz to secure vital crude oil and energy cargoes amid ongoing disruptions. The government is finalizing plans for Indian vessels to navigate the waterway, which handles a significant portion of global oil flows, as shipping activity has slowed due to the Iran conflict.

title: Goenka's advice to Sooryavanshi: 'Preserve innocence… the world will be at your feet'
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 93 off 38 balls powered Rajasthan Royals to a seven-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2026. The teenage sensation's remarkable knock, featuring 10 sixes, saw him become the first Indian to hit 50 sixes in a season. LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka personally congratulated the young star, acknowledging his immense talent and maturity.

title: 'Economic storm brewing & ...': Rahul takes 'nautanki' swipe at PM 's gift to Meloni
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi for gifting toffees to Italian counterpart Giorgia Meloni in Rome, while accusing him of "laughing and filming reels" amid the struggles of farmers, youth, women, workers, and small traders in India. Gandhi deemed the gesture "a farce" and not leadership, contrasting it with an "economic storm" brewing domestically.

title: 'No work for 3 years': UP BJP councillor shaves her head in protest
content: A BJP councillor in Firozabad shaved her head at the municipal corporation to protest alleged discrimination in development works and neglect of civic issues in her ward. Usha Devi Shankhwar claims the mayor's husband is obstructing projects, a charge the Municipal Commissioner denies, stating significant work is underway.

title: Watch: PM Modi's surprise gift for Giorgia Meloni has a very 'Melody' twist
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni shared a video thanking Prime Minister Narendra Modi for gifting her 'Melody' chocolates. The term, a blend of their surnames, gained popularity during their previous meetings. This latest viral moment occurred during Modi's visit to Rome, focused on strengthening India-Italy ties across various sectors.

title: ‘We are humans too, we make mistakes’: Parag urges IPL commentators to ‘talk about cricket’
content: Rajasthan Royals skipper Riyan Parag urged commentators and experts to cease personal criticism and focus solely on cricket. He emphasised players' hard work and preparation, highlighting that setbacks don't negate their efforts. Parag stressed the importance of respecting the sport and its athletes, advocating for discussions centered on the game itself.

title: India and Italy agree to elevate ties to special strategic partnership
content: Ahead of bilateral talks, PM Modi called on Italian President Sergio Mattarella and discussed different aspects relating to India-Italy friendship, including trade, investment and cultural linkages

title: Kerala Cabinet 2026: Full list of Ministers
content: The detailed portfolios will be submitted to the Governor and will be published via gazette notification

title: ‘Has double-engine govt run out of oil?’ Opposition questions Maharashtra Govt over fuel shortage reports
content: Congress Legislature Party leader Vijay Wadettiwar and NCP (SP) MLA Jayant Patil cited incidents where a police vehicle sent to search for a missing girl in Nagpur apparently did not have diesel, and attack on a petrol pump worker in Jalgaon over alleged fuel shortage

title: Hormuz crisis: Australian LNG to India will surge, says Australian High Commissioner
content: Australian LNG could reach eastern India through a crisis-free Indian Ocean route, the High Commissioner says; lithium and copper may follow for the clean-energy shift

title: Politicising Zubeen Garg's death backfired on Congress-led Opposition in Assam polls: CM Himanta Biswa Sarma
content: The 52-year-old singer had gone to the Southeast Asian nation to perform in the 4th edition of the festival

title: T.N. CM Vijay pays tribute to social reformer Pandit Iyothee Thass; launches operation of 40 new vehicles for DMRHS
content: Mr. Vijay, along with his Cabinet colleagues, paid tributes to the social reformer on his 181st birth anniversary

title: Bengaluru delivery executive arrested for sexually assaulting cow in Cottonpet
content: The incident came to light after CCTV footage of the act went viral on social media, triggering public outrage and demands for strict action against the accused.

title: TVK extends open invitation to VCK, IUML to be part of Tamil Nadu Cabinet
content: “We invite VCK leader Thol. Thirumavalavan to be part of the Council of Ministers. This is the wish of the Chief Minister as well. Similarly, the IUML should also join the Council of Ministers,” Minister Aadhav Arjuna told reporters in Chennai.

title: Decision on joining TVK-led Tamil Nadu Cabinet soon: VCK chief Thirumavalavan
content: Speaking to reporters after TVK leader and Public Works Minister Aadhav Arjuna gave an open invitation to the VCK to join the Cabinet, Mr. Thirumavalavan said, “I will discuss this with frontline leaders and take a call on the way forward.”

title: Delhi Police suggest larger Supreme Court Bench to review UAPA bail restrictions
content: Additional Solicitor General S.V. Raju said the apex court’s May 18 ruling overlooked that under the UAPA’s statutory bail bar, the presumption of innocence takes a ‘backseat’

title: IUML will decide on joining Cabinet on May 21: Kader Mohideen
content: “We have no issues with the AIADMK rebel faction [joining the Cabinet]. We will withdraw support if BJP is included in the Cabinet”

title: Population incentives reflect need for debate on low fertility rates: Nara Lokesh
content: “We felt that this is the right way to create a mechanism where families that choose to have more children are supported by the State,” says IT & Electronics and HRD Minister Nara Lokesh.

title: Civil rights bodies, Christian groups flay ‘murder drama’ enacted by self-styled pastor
content: The CRII, APCLF, and TUCPA have demanded accountability and strict legal action, warning that fabricated incidents like the alleged “murder drama” threaten communal harmony and exploit vulnerable communities.

title: India and Italy agree to elevate ties to special strategic partnership | LIVE
content: India-Italy joint strategic action plan 2025-2029 gives our ties a practical and futuristic framework, says PM Modi

title: SC flags ‘woeful’ shortage of Rajasthan forest staff amid Chambal mining crackdown
content: Top court says acute understaffing in Forest Department a ‘serious’ concern given Rajasthan’s vast and ecologically significant forest cover

title: Supreme Court suspects Karnataka government of ‘delaying tactics’ but extends Bengaluru civic polls’ deadline to August 31
content: The apex court said this would be the State government’s ‘last chance’, and there would be no further extensions

title: Karnataka state police chief issues guidelines to strengthen engagement with retired personnel
content: Karnataka DG-IGP M.A. Saleem said that retired officers and staff could significantly contribute to community policing by sharing information related to anti-social activities, drug peddling, smuggling and other unlawful activities in the areas where they reside.

title: Thripunithura firecracker blast: ₹10 lakh each relief to families of victims
content: Compensation sanctioned from Chief Minister’s Distress Relief Fund to families of Vishnu A. of Ulloor, Thiruvananthapuram, and Anil S. of Shooranad, Kollam, who were killed in the explosion at a temporary firecracker storage unit in February 2024

title: Naidu attends Gangamamba Jatara at Kuppam, shares roadside ice apples with toddy tappers
content: The Chief Minister and Nara Bhuvaneswari offer silk robes at the temple, then halt by the road for thati munjalu he calls free of adulteration and carbide

title: Madras High Court dismisses challenge to Tamil Nadu government’s decision to close down 717 liquor shops
content: Justice G.R. Swaminathan commends the decision taken by the government and tells Advocate General Vijay Narayan that it is a “good decision”

title: Southwest Airlines opens GCC in Hyderabad, to hire 1,000 people
content: Executive VP & chief information officer, Southwest Airlines, said Hyderabad is one of the deepest analytics, engineering, and innovation talent pools in the world, making it an ideal location for the company

title: Union Minister Bandi Sanjay alleges ‘smear campaign’ by BRS, denies rumours
content: Union Minister of State for Home Affairs Bandi Sanjay Kumar dismisses rumours about his impending arrest, removal from the Union Cabinet, or the possibility of launching a new political party

title: Class 10 Board exam results: Tamil Nadu records 94.3% pass rate
content: While Pudukottai topped the State with a pass percentage of 97.57%, Sivaganga came second with 97.54%, followed by Thanjavur at 97.41%

title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain PIL for protection, audit of Sikh religious properties
content: It also sought a direction to the Comptroller and Auditor General of India to conduct a special audit of all statutory bodies, boards, committees and trusts managing Sikh religious properties

title: OCI status of foreigners who voted in Tamil Nadu Assembly election comes under scrutiny
content: The number of foreigners of Indian origin who voted in a fraudulent manner has increased to 30. Their declaration while applying for the OCI card that they had surrendered their voter identity cards or deleted their names from the electoral rolls will be treated as a false declaration and action will taken under law, sources say

title: Medha Kulkarni appointed new chairperson of parliamentary panel on Science, Environment
content: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has also appointed BJP member Sudheer Gupta as chairperson of the Joint Committee on Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026

title: How Karunanidhi headed a minority government in Tamil Nadu for five years
content: The last time Tamil Nadu witnessed a coalition government was in 1952; however, leaders of the Dravidian parties largely steered clear of the idea thereafter

title: Delhi HC sets aside order staying FIR registration against Abhijit Iyer Mitra
content: While dealing with a petition by Newslaundry editorial director Manisha Pande against the stay order by the sessions court, Justice Girish Kathpalia noted that the stay order was passed without any reasons and remarked, "This kind of stay does not convince."

title: Woman dies after man sets her on fire in Aruppukottai
content: P. Shanthi, who was widowed, was pressured by her late husband’s friend into moving in with him. When she refused, he set her on fire, according to the police

title: Philanthropy Asia Summit: Asian leaders push for digital infrastructure, strong regional ties
content: The Philanthropy Asia Alliance emphasised the need to move beyond traditional, family and charity-based philanthropy towards more institutional and collaborative models for addressing common problems in Asian countries

title: Rajya Sabha MP receives death threat on WhatsApp, FIR lodged
content: Ramji Lal Suman alleged that he had earlier received threats several times through social media platforms

title: From ‘virus’ to ‘tsunami’: How outgoing Chief Ministers, leaders of Tamil Nadu viewed election results since 1967
content: In a recent comment on the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam winning the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections, DMK president M.K. Stalin described the victory as a “new and attractive tsunami of illusion”

title: India must be alert amid West Asia crisis, no immediate worry: Civil Aviation Minister Naidu
content: Kinjarapu Rammohan Naidu also announced that Jharkhand’s Dumka, Hazaribag, Chaibasa, Daltonganj and some other districts will get air connectivity soon

title: Hyderabad | Strike shuts thousands of medical stores on May 20
content: People in Hyderabad search for stores to buy medicines. Pharmacies in hospitals, corporate pharmacy chains remained open

title: Constitution, voice of Gandhi, Ambedkar, under attack: Rahul in Raebareli
content: Rahul Gandhi alleged that the Prime Minister is "attacking the Constitution 24 hours a day" by weakening MNREGA, not conducting caste census, and favouring select industrialists

title: Tamil Nadu Class 10 results: Chennai Corporation schools record 89% pass percentage
content: According to data from the Chennai Corporation’s Education Department, 6,271 of the 7,081 students who appeared for the examination cleared it

title: Tamil producer K. Rajan passes away
content: Rajan was best known for producing films such ‘Thangamana Thangachi’ (1991) and ‘Chinna Poovai Killathe’ (1992) and directing films like ‘Unarchigal’ (2005)

title: Kerala’s UDF government announces 100-day action plan from June 1
content: Directives issued to all departments to take necessary steps for implementation of all promises in UDF’s election manifesto by 2031

title: Telangana heatwave | CM urges people not to travel in afternoons
content: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy directs officials to ensure availability of adequate ORS packets, supply of drinking water and uninterrupted power supply to residents

title: A Gooseberry and a Tear: The other crucial Straits of the Indian Ocean
content: While the Strait of Hormuz remains a global flashpoint, two more Indian Ocean chokepoints are equally crucial for global commerce.

title: Indian Railways approves ₹993-crore Arakkonam-Chengalpattu track doubling project
content: The sanctioning comes in the wake of the Southern Railway conducting the survey work for acquiring land last year.

title: Centre willing to proceed with HMR Phase II on 50:50 cost-sharing basis: Khattar
content: Under the Centre’s AMRUT 2.0 scheme, 39 sewage treatment plants (STPs) were sanctioned to treat wastewater flowing into the Musi, says Union Minister for Housing and Urban Affairs

title: Storage room near TASMAC outlet catches fire in Nagapattinam district
content: The incident has drawn attention, as local residents and women had staged protests in recent weeks demanding the removal of the liquor outlet, alleging inconvenience to the public

title: Forum appeals Centre to continue teaching French in Puducherry schools
content: The new guidelines giving importance to the teaching of two Indian languages would deprive the students of Puducherry the opportunity of learning French.

title: K.V. Thomas quits as Kerala’s Special Representative in New Delhi after new UDF govt takes over
content: Thomas clarified that his tenure naturally came to an end when the term of former Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan ended

title: N.S.K. Umesh to be Kerala Chief Minister’s Officer on Special Duty
content: Umesh will continue to hold the post of Director of General Education till another person is appointed

title: Twisha Sharma death: NCW seeks report, details on accused husband
content: Police have announced a cash reward of ₹10,000 for information leading to the arrest of the husband, who has been absconding

title: Madras High Court summons former Chennai Police Commissioner Arun in Goondas Act case against real estate developer
content: Judges say the branding of a real estate developer as a ‘goonda,’ on the basis of a complaint lodged by DMDK Rajya Sabha member L.K. Sudhish’s wife, ‘shocks their conscience’

title: Increasing crimes against minors in Karnataka highlighted by women’s commission chairperson
content: Karnataka State Women’s Commission Chairperson Nagalakshmi Choudhary has said that crimes against minors had continue despite enacting several laws

title: BJP govt. in State will be of nationalists, says CM Suvendu during maiden visit to north Bengal
content: BJP government will work towards giving north Bengal its developmental dues to erase the deprivation meted out to the region earlier, says Suvendu Adhikari during his maiden visit to the region

title: Every institution must implement safety measures for women in workplace, says Karnataka women’s commission chairperson
content: She cited the increase in instances of sexual harassment, sexual abuse, and mental harassment against women at workplaces

title: Joint military exercise of 13 nations begins in Meghalaya, focus on counter-terrorism operations
content: The multilateral military exercise 'PRAGATI 2026' aims at enabling seamless coordination among participating nations in joint operations, identifying common areas of cooperation and sharing expertise

title: Delhi experiences warm morning; 'orange' alert issued for heatwave
content: An ‘orange’ alert stands for 'be prepared', while the maximum temperature is expected to hover around 46 degrees Celsius

title: Pune firm successfully tests 300km-range Suryastra rockets; Army procuring launcher systems
content: NIBE Limited successfully carried out the firing demonstration of the EXTRA and Predator Hawk “Suryastra” rockets from the Integrated Test Range (ITR) in Chandipur, Odisha . both rockets achieved highly accurate strikes during testing, with a circular error probable (CEP), a standard measure of missile accuracy, of just 1.5 m and 2 m respectively.

content: Stalin has formed a committee to identify the reasons behind the party's poor performance. The DMK is also expected to undertake major organisational changes in the coming months.

title: Teesta And Ganga: How The Two Rivers Are Key To India-Bangladesh Ties
content: Teesta Water Agreement had been held up since 2011 due to opposition from Mamata Banerjee, and the Ganga Water Agreement is up for renewal this year

title: "Ready To Take On Terror": J&K Women Police Recruits' Message To Pakistan
content: Among the 100 recruits who received appointment letters from Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha on Monday, around 60 per cent were women.

content: He further said he worked with simplicity, championing transparency and standing firmly against corruption under his tenure as cabinet minister and Uttarakhand Chief Minister.

content: The charge sheet identifies Addepalli Janardhana Rao and his brother Addepalli Jagan Mohan Rao, residents of Ibrahimpatnam in NTR district, as the main accused.

content: The CRS, which provides policy and legal analysis to the US Congress and committees, compiled the losses by perusing news reports and statements by the Department of Defence and the US Central Command (CENTCOM).

content: The leaders said the two countries "aim to create an open, trustworthy and equitable digital space in which every nation can shape and benefit from Al".

content: He also said that leaders such as Indira Gandhi, P V Narasimha Rao and Manmohan Singh always kept the country's future and reputation at the centre of their leadership.

title: "I Cry In Shower": Meta, LinkedIn Employees Share Fears, Anxiety Amid Layoffs
content: Life decisions are on hold. Employees say they are unable to plan a future because they don't know if they will still have a salary next quarter.

content: Ashok Kharat is facing 12 cases of sexual exploitation and financial fraud in Nashik and Ahilyanagar.

content: Internet users often use the "Melodi" hashtag to describe the friendly equation between the two leaders.

title: Vijay Drives Bolero, Flags Off Fleet Of 40 Vehicles For Health Department
content: Vijay inaugurated the services of 40 new vehicles for the Department of Health and Public Welfare worth Rs 2.90 crore.

content: Twisha Sharma's death came barely five months after her marriage to Samarth Singh, a lawyer. The victim was 33 years old.

title: 'Traffic, meetings till 10pm': Man who moved from Germany to Bengaluru on how life changed
content: a man living in Bengaluru shared how his daily routine changed more than he imagined after moving to the busy tech city from Germany . he says he wakes up to car and truck noise and walks inside a gated society . it took him 1.5 hours in traffic to reach the office where more meetings & brainstorming took place .
